title: The skeleton of a page
assignment: Review the concepts from this section. If something is unclear, go back to the lesson.
groups.0.title: The two halves
groups.0.items.0.def: <html> wraps everything. Inside are two halves: <head> is the invisible one (information ABOUT the page), <body> is the visible one (the page itself).
groups.0.items.1.def: Everything that is ABOUT the page but is not the page: the tab title, the charset, the language. A reader does not see it — machines read it.
groups.0.items.2.def: Everything a person sees: headings, text, images. If it shows on screen — it goes here.
groups.1.title: The lines everybody writes
groups.1.items.0.def: The first line of the file. It says "this is modern HTML". Without it the browser switches into a mode for ancient sites and some things go crooked.
groups.1.items.1.def: The table that turns letters back from numbers. Without utf-8 non-English letters come out as question marks on someone else's computer. The code works — it just lies.
groups.1.items.2.def: An attribute on <html>. It says what language the page is in. A blind person's reader needs to know — otherwise it reads the text in the wrong voice and it comes out as gibberish.
groups.2.title: More from this section
groups.2.items.0.def: The name of the tab. Bookmarks, history, the blue line in Google — all of them read it. None of them looks at your page.
groups.2.items.1.def: Attributes on . src says which image. alt says what is seen on it — for a blind person and for Google. "photo" is not alt; alt is what replaces the picture when it is gone.
groups.2.items.2.def: <!-- a note -->. Nobody sees it except whoever opens the code — usually you, six months later. But be careful: commented-out code does NOT exist. To the browser it is gone.
